Auditorium

POPL 2026

/!\ We are aware of a scam travel company reaching out to participants. They are in no way affiliated to POPL, please be careful and always contact the organizers in case of doubt. /!\

Welcome to the website of the 53rd ACM SIGPLAN Symposium on Principles of Programming Languages (POPL 2026). POPL 2026 will take place in Rennes, France.

See the Call For Paper for detailed information.

Latest Announcements

Social events on Thursday morning

Rennes Métropole (City of Rennes) is pleased to invite you to two activities on Thursday, January 15:

  • Guided walking tour of the historic center of Rennes from 9:00 a.m. to 10:00 a.m., or

  • Lecture on the history of the Jacobins Convent from 9:30 to 9:50 a.m. in the Nef room.

Please come to the registration desk if you want to participate to the guided tour.

Proceedings

The proceedings for CPP, POPL and PEPM have been published in the ACM digital library.

The annual Symposium on Principles of Programming Languages is a forum for the discussion of all aspects of programming languages and programming systems. Both theoretical and experimental papers are welcome on topics ranging from formal frameworks to experience reports. We seek submissions that make principled, enduring contributions to the theory, design, understanding, implementation, or application of programming languages.

The symposium is sponsored by ACM SIGPLAN, in cooperation with ACM SIGACT and ACM SIGLOG.

Live streams of some talks will be available on the YouTube SIGPLAN channel.
